steps.title: Extract the key finding from the passage
steps.content:
The researchers found that glossy ibises prefer deep water during breeding season, but small dark herons do not share that preference. Water management strategies that increase water depth are therefore more likely to benefit glossy ibises than small dark herons. Evaluate each choice against this finding.
  • Choice A — attract glossy ibises to the area than they are to attract small dark herons to the area.: Deep water favors glossy ibises and not herons, so strategies that increase depth are more likely to attract ibises. This follows directly from the research. ✓
  • Choice B — attract birds that don't typically live in wetlands to the area than they are to attract glossy ibises or small dark herons to the area.: The passage is entirely about wetland birds; it says nothing about non-wetland species. ✗
  • Choice C — extend the average lifespan of glossy ibises already in the area than they are to attract a greater number of those birds to the area.: Lifespan is never discussed in the passage; this choice introduces information not in the text. ✗
  • Choice D — decrease the area's appeal to both glossy ibises and small dark herons than they are to increase the appeal to either.: This contradicts the finding that deeper water increases appeal for glossy ibises. ✗

steps.title: Confirm the match
steps.content:
Only Choice A — attract glossy ibises to the area than they are to attract small dark herons to the area. correctly reflects the research conclusion: deeper water disproportionately attracts glossy ibises, not small dark herons.

That matches Choice A — attract glossy ibises to the area than they are to attract small dark herons to the area..
